assertion_results.text: Step 8 Case A creates standard remediation tasks for the current stream (rhtpa-2.2) with standard labels ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123'] and 'Depend' link to TC-8020
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Case A creates two standard tasks for rhtpa-2.2 (upstream backport and downstream propagation). Both use labels ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123'] (lines 25 and 96). Both are linked to TC-8020 with type 'Depend' (lines 78-83 and 143-148). Summary table at lines 322-324 confirms: 'Upstream backport (rhtpa-2.2)' and 'Downstream propagation (rhtpa-2.2)' both have standard labels and Depend link.

assertion_results.text: Step 8 Case B detects that stream rhtpa-2.1 is also affected but has no CVE Jira — creates proactive preemptive remediation tasks (§1.60)
assertion_results.evidence: cross-stream.md confirms JQL search found no sibling CVE Jira for rhtpa-2.1 (line 22: 'No sibling Vulnerability issues found'). version-impact.md confirms rhtpa-2.1 is affected (tokio 1.40.0 < 1.42.0, lines 9-10). remediation.md Case B (lines 158-315) creates preemptive remediation tasks for rhtpa-2.1. The output explicitly labels these as 'Preemptive' type in the summary table (lines 325-326).

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks for stream rhtpa-2.1 include the 'security-preemptive' label alongside standard labels: ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123', 'security-preemptive']
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 175 and 251 show Jira creation calls with labels: ['ai-generated-jira', 'Security', 'CVE-2026-55123', 'security-preemptive'] for both preemptive upstream backport and downstream propagation tasks. cross-stream.md line 30 confirms: 'Labels: ai-generated-jira, Security, CVE-2026-55123, security-preemptive'. Summary table lines 325-326 also show all four labels.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive tasks are linked to the originating CVE Jira TC-8020 with 'Related' link type (not 'Depend') because the originating CVE belongs to a different stream
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 237-239 show upstream preemptive task linked to TC-8020 with type 'Related'. Lines 306-309 show downstream preemptive task also linked to TC-8020 with type 'Related'. cross-stream.md line 31 explicitly states: 'Link type to TC-8020: Related (not Depend, because the originating CVE belongs to a different stream)'. Summary table lines 325-326 confirm 'Related' link type for both preemptive tasks.

assertion_results.text: Preemptive task descriptions include a preemptive remediation prefix noting TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2) as the originating CVE
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 192-195 (upstream preemptive task) and lines 268-271 (downstream preemptive task) both include the prefix: '> **Preemptive remediation**: This task was created proactively from cross-stream > impact analysis of TC-8020 (stream rhtpa-2.2). > No stream-specific CVE Jira exists yet for this stream. When PSIRT creates one, > this task will be linked and the `security-preemptive` label removed.' This prefix explicitly references TC-8020 and stream rhtpa-2.2 as the originating CVE. cross-stream.md lines 43-48 also confirm this prefix template.
